mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals imminent discount-rate relief and easier financial conditions, lifting equity multiples and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines but 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of such observations, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% while CPIAUCSL inflation is still elevated, the market should price continued restrictive policy and lower near-term equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation remains positive
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered observations produce positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power versus single-series thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and weaker earnings expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if upward UNRATE trends are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or if the sign of the rolling 3-year correlation flips persistently positive.

mechanism_narrative: A threshold increase in PAYEMS, especially when unemployment is stable or falling, indicates payroll momentum that supports consumption and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises by more than 0.2% over 2 months while UNRATE does not increase
failure_condition: Reject if the payroll-growth trigger does not outperform unconditional returns, or if triggered 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, which should support equity earnings expectations and lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations, or if the signal's Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO weakens while HOUST also falls, the joint signal points to synchronized real-economy contraction and deteriorating earnings breadth, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined decline in INDPRO and HOUST is not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if either series alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST below its recent trend signals housing-cycle stress and weaker future demand, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the housing-drop trigger is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of episodes, or if the effect disappears after controlling for general market trend.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 together with improving RSAFS confirms broad final-demand rebound, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through higher earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the signal fails outside recovery regimes.
